title: Remediate: _ToolClient Protocol defined in adapters instead of ports
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/adapters/kanban_board.py:38, src/hermes_attractor/adapters/event_log.py:46

**Finding**:
Both `HermesKanbanBoard` and `HermesEventLog` adapters define an identical `_ToolClient(Protocol)` inline (private, file-local). Port contracts (Protocols / ABCs) belong in `ports/`, not in `adapters/`. The current approach duplicates the interface definition and makes it impossible to reference the contract from outside the adapter without importing the adapter itself, which would invert the dependency arrow.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Add `src/hermes_attractor/ports/hermes_tool_client.py` (or extend `ports/hermes.py`) with a public `HermesToolClient` Protocol.
2. Remove the private `_ToolClient` definitions from both adapter files.
3. Update `HermesKanbanBoard.__init__` and `HermesEventLog.__init__` to accept the public port type.
4. Update any tests that construct these adapters to use/assert the public type.

**Acceptance**:
- `_ToolClient` no longer appears in any `adapters/` file.
- A single `HermesToolClient` Protocol exists in `ports/`.
- Both adapters import the Protocol from `ports/` (not inline).
- All tests pass at 100% coverage.
close_reason: Extracted HermesToolClient Protocol from adapters into ports/hermes_tool_client.py; both adapters now import from ports

title: Remediate: run query logic in plugin/tools.py handle_attractor_status and handle_attractor_result
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/plugin/tools.py:358-378, src/hermes_attractor/plugin/tools.py:397-410

**Finding**:
`handle_attractor_status` and `handle_attractor_result` in `plugin/tools.py` contain query/projection logic — filtering nodes by `status.value in ("RUNNING", "DISPATCHED")` and extracting `run.context.data` — directly in the plugin layer. The plugin is the composition root and should only wire adapters and call use cases; application logic (even simple projections over domain objects) belongs in `use_cases/`.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Add `query_run_status(run_id, run_state: RunStateStore) -> dict` to `use_cases/run_execution.py` (or a new `use_cases/query.py`).
2. Add `query_run_result(run_id, run_state: RunStateStore) -> dict` similarly.
3. Reduce `handle_attractor_status` and `handle_attractor_result` in `plugin/tools.py` to wiring + a single use-case call each.

**Acceptance**:
- `plugin/tools.py` handlers contain no node-filter or context-projection logic.
- The status/result query functions live in `use_cases/` and are unit-tested.
- All tests pass at 100% coverage.
close_reason: query_run_status and query_run_result added to use_cases/run_execution.py; handlers thinned to wire-only; 100% coverage

title: Remediate: TOOL-node coverage gaps in run_execution and reconcile
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/use_cases/run_execution.py, src/hermes_attractor/use_cases/reconcile.py

**Finding**:
The test suite fails the 100% branch-coverage gate (99.47%). Two coverage gaps exist on pre-existing code:

1. run_execution.py lines 506-507: The branch where tool_registry is not None, tool_name is non-empty, AND tool_registry.run() returns a non-dict value (the cast fallback to {}) is never exercised.
2. run_execution.py lines 511-512: The branch where tool_result has a 'context_updates' key whose value IS a dict (triggering context.apply()) is never exercised. Existing tests only cover the else-branch (no context_updates).
3. reconcile.py line 129: The early-return branch when event_log.read_since() returns an empty sequence is not covered. All existing reconcile tests that call _reconcile_run directly provide non-empty event lists.

**Fix suggestion**:
Add targeted unit tests:
- test_advance_tool_with_registry_returning_non_dict_uses_empty_result: pass a tool_registry mock where run() returns a non-dict (e.g. a string); assert run_state.save_run called with unchanged context.
- test_advance_tool_with_context_updates_applies_updates: pass a tool_registry mock where run() returns {'context_updates': {'key': 'val'}}; assert the saved run context has the merged key.
- test_reconcile_run_does_nothing_when_events_empty: call _reconcile_run (imported via the module or through reconcile with a run that has events returning []) with event_log.read_since returning []; assert store.load not called.

**Acceptance**:
- uv run pytest --cov passes at 100% with branch coverage on
- No new pragma: no cover added
close_reason: Duplicate of hermes-attractor-zym.13 (identical code-quality finding, inadvertently double-filed)

title: Remediate: type: ignore used instead of pyright: ignore in guard.py and tools.py
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/domain/guard.py, src/hermes_attractor/plugin/tools.py

**Finding**:
CLAUDE.md mandates: 'Use # pyright: ignore[reportX] (not # type: ignore) when a suppression is genuinely needed — reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment is an error.' Four lines in guard.py and one in tools.py use the disallowed # type: ignore form:
- guard.py:275: bool(left < right)  # type: ignore[operator]
- guard.py:277: bool(left <= right)  # type: ignore[operator]
- guard.py:279: bool(left > right)  # type: ignore[operator]
- guard.py:281: bool(left >= right)  # type: ignore[operator]
- tools.py:236: StyleRule(...)  # type: ignore[index]

These will cause ruff or pyright to emit reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment errors if the project enables strict mypy/pyright cross-checking and the errors disappear in a future pyright version, leaving stale ignore comments.

**Fix suggestion**:
Replace each # type: ignore[operator] with # pyright: ignore[reportOperatorIssue] in guard.py and # type: ignore[index] with # pyright: ignore[reportArgumentType] (or the exact pyright error code) in tools.py. Run uv run pyright to confirm the correct error code, then replace.

**Acceptance**:
- grep -r 'type: ignore' src/ returns no matches
- uv run pyright passes at 0 errors
- uv run ruff check . passes
close_reason: replaced 5 disallowed type: ignore comments with pyright: ignore[reportX] in guard.py and tools.py

title: Remediate: domain unit test imports PydotSerializer adapter
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: tests/unit/domain/test_sp_pipeline.py:12

**Finding**:
tests/unit/domain/test_sp_pipeline.py imports PydotSerializer from the adapters layer (line 12: `from hermes_attractor.adapters.dot_serializer import PydotSerializer`). Domain unit tests should depend only on the domain; importing an adapter couples the domain test suite to the pydot third-party library and makes failures in the adapter contaminate domain test results.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Move tests/unit/domain/test_sp_pipeline.py to tests/integration/ (rename to test_sp_pipeline_integration.py or similar), since the tests exercise the real DOT parse path (adapter + domain together).
2. Update the pytestmark if the integration suite uses a different marker.
3. Ensure 100% coverage is maintained after the move.

**Acceptance**:
- No file under tests/unit/domain/ imports from hermes_attractor.adapters or hermes_attractor.plugin.
- The sp-workflow.dot parse tests still run and pass.
- ruff + pyright clean, 100% coverage maintained.

tests/unit/use_cases/test_run_execution.py imports and tests three plugin-layer handlers (`handle_attractor_run`, `handle_attractor_status`, `handle_attractor_result`) — import at line 27 and test functions at lines 372, 400, 748. **Acceptance**:

title: Remediate: handle_attractor_add_node returns spec_id instead of shape in response
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/plugin/tools.py:189

**Finding**:
`handle_attractor_add_node` returns `{"spec_id": spec_id, "node_id": node_id, "shape": updated.spec_id}` at line 189. `updated` is a `Pipeline` object; `updated.spec_id` returns the pipeline identifier string (e.g. `"flow"`), not the NodeShape name. The `shape` field in the response is therefore always wrong — it echoes the pipeline spec_id instead of the node's shape string (e.g. `"START"`). Tests only assert `payload["ok"] is True`, missing the shape field content.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Change line 189 from `"shape": updated.spec_id` to `"shape": shape_str` (the shape_str local variable already holds the correct NodeShape name).
2. Add a test assertion: `assert payload["result"]["shape"] == "START"` (or whichever shape was passed) to `test_handle_attractor_create_and_add_node_ok`.
3. Run `uv run pytest` to confirm 100% coverage still holds.

**Acceptance**:
- `handle_attractor_add_node` returns `{"shape": <NodeShape-name>}` (not the spec_id) in the result payload.
- Test explicitly asserts the `shape` field value matches the input shape argument.
- ruff + pyright clean, 100% coverage maintained.
close_reason: fix handle_attractor_add_node to return shape_str instead of pipeline spec_id; test asserts shape field

title: Remediate: path confinement uses prefix match in GitPipelineStore
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/adapters/pipeline_store.py:75

**Finding**:
Path confinement uses a prefix string match (str(resolved).startswith(str(repo_root_resolved))). A sibling directory such as /repo-evil satisfies the prefix for base /repo, so the defense-in-depth confinement check is incorrect (weak pattern). The _SAFE_SPEC_ID_RE regex currently masks exploitation, but the fallback symlink-escape guard is wrong.

**Fix suggestion**:
Replace startswith with Path.is_relative_to: 'if not resolved.is_relative_to(repo_root_resolved):'. Keep the regex check as the first line of defense.

**Acceptance**:
- pipeline_store.py uses resolved.is_relative_to(repo_root_resolved) for confinement
- A test proves a sibling dir like /repo-evil is rejected (not a prefix bypass)
- ruff + pyright clean, 100% coverage maintained
close_reason: replaced startswith with is_relative_to for path confinement; added sibling-dir regression test; 100% coverage maintained

title: Remediate: unconfined repo_path arg in attractor tools _make_store
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/plugin/tools.py:54-65

**Finding**:
_make_store takes the repo_path tool argument (caller/LLM-controlled) and uses it directly as the GitPipelineStore root with no validation or confinement. ensure_repo() then runs 'git init', writes a .dot file, and git-commits into whatever directory the caller names (including absolute paths or paths containing ..). This lets a malicious or buggy caller initialize a git repo and write/commit files into an arbitrary writable location.

**Fix suggestion**:
Validate/confine repo_path before constructing GitPipelineStore: resolve it and require it to be relative-to a configured allowed base (e.g. cwd or an env-configured root) via Path.is_relative_to; reject absolute paths and '..' segments otherwise. Return a structured error (handlers already wrap in _safe) when rejected.

**Acceptance**:
- repo_path is validated/confined before any git or filesystem operation
- A test proves an out-of-base repo_path is rejected with an error payload (no git init / no file write)
- ruff + pyright clean, 100% coverage maintained
close_reason: repo_path is validated/confined via RepoPathConfinementError; three rejection paths and symlink escape tested; 100% coverage maintained

title: Remediate: DOT attribute injection in PydotSerializer.emit
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/hermes_attractor/adapters/dot_serializer.py:180-202

**Finding**:
emit() wraps attribute values as f'"{value}"' (profile/prompt/class/condition/label) without escaping embedded double-quotes or backslashes. A value containing a '"' breaks out of the DOT attribute, producing malformed/injected DOT and a non-round-trippable graph. Untrusted prompt/condition text flows here from authoring tool args.

**Note (informational, no separate task)**: parse() enforces the byte-size cap before calling pydot.graph_from_dot_data, but node/edge caps are only checked after the full input is parsed. Given the 1 MiB cap this is acceptable; documenting the parse-then-check ordering is sufficient.

**Fix suggestion**:
Escape backslash and double-quote in each attribute value before interpolation (replace '\\' then '"'), or build pydot.Node/Edge with raw values and let pydot perform quoting instead of pre-quoting.

**Acceptance**:
- Attribute values containing '"' and '\\' round-trip through emit() -> parse() unchanged
- A test covers a prompt/condition containing an embedded double quote
- ruff + pyright clean, 100% coverage maintained
close_reason: DOT attribute injection fixed by escaping backslash and double-quote in emit(); 100% coverage maintained
